pattern: BFS or DFS traversal of an implicit employee tree, accumulating importance values for the target and all their descendants.
hints: Build a map from employee ID to their record so you can look up any employee in O(1) during traversal.
Start from the given ID and expand outward through subordinate lists — a queue or recursive call works the same way.
Sum each visited employee's importance value; there are no cycles (each employee has at most one direct leader).

edge_inputs.description: three-node tree with manager and two direct subordinates
query on a leaf with negative importance
query starting at root gives total of all
deep chain where importance accumulates across levels
query on middle of chain — only partial subtree included

stress.description: chain of employees where root importance query sums all
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    # build a chain: 1->2->3->...->n
    employees = []
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        imp = rng.randint(-100, 100)
        subs = [i + 1] if i < n else []
        employees.append([i, imp, subs])
    return [employees, 1]
stress.note: chain structure tests full traversal depth
